message:
refactor(database): migrate SQLite operations from blocking std::sync::mpsc to async oneshot channels

Replace std::sync::mpsc + handle.wait() with crate::channel::oneshot in
all SQLite connection methods (open, open_in_memory, execute,
execute_batch, query) to properly integrate with the async runtime and
support cooperative cancellation.

Each operation now:
- Creates an asupersync oneshot channel instead of std::sync::mpsc
- Reserves a permit before spawning the blocking task
- Awaits the oneshot result instead of blocking on handle.wait() + rx.recv()
- Handles RecvError::Cancelled by cancelling the pool handle and returning
  Outcome::Cancelled

SqliteTransaction::Drop no longer calls handle.wait() on rollback -- it is
now fire-and-forget (best-effort) to avoid blocking the async executor
during drop, which could cause deadlocks in single-threaded runtimes.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
